name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans up to $1,000, and title loans up to $25,000 with same-day funding at physical locations and online.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized lender specializing in short-term emergency cash solutions. The company operates physical store locations across multiple states, including a Jacksonville, FL branch at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave., and provides online lending options. With a reported 4.9 out of 5 stars across 125,419 reviews, the company has established brand recognition in the subprime lending market.

Advance America offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans ($100–$500 for 2–4 week terms), Installment Loans ($100–$1,000 with multiple repayment options), and Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000 secured by vehicle equity). Applicants can apply online or in-store, with the Jacksonville location offering bilingual Spanish-language service. The company also provides Western Union money transfer services. Requirements include government-issued ID, proof of income, an active checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN.

Advance America differentiates itself through multi-channel access (online and in-store), flexible loan products serving different borrower needs, same-day funding availability, and established customer service infrastructure. The company emphasizes staff training and personalized loan selection assistance, as reflected in customer testimonials. Store hours extend to 6 PM weekdays and 4 PM Saturdays, accommodating working consumers.

While Advance America provides accessible credit to underbanked populations, borrowers should note that payday and title loans typically carry high APRs (not disclosed on this page) and create short repayment cycles that can lead to debt cycles. The company is a commercial lender, not a nonprofit credit counselor, and operates within the high-cost lending sector. Installment loans offer longer terms than payday loans, potentially reducing rollover risk.
pros: Same-day funding available for payday and installment loans
Multiple loan products (payday, installment, title) accommodate different borrower circumstances
High loan ceilings for title loans ($25,000) based on vehicle equity
Online and in-store application options for convenience
Bilingual Spanish-language support at physical locations
Flexible repayment terms on installment loans versus fixed 2–4 week payday cycles
4.9-star rating across 125,419 reviews indicating consistent customer satisfaction
cons: APR rates not disclosed on website; high-cost lending structure typical of payday/title loans
Payday loans capped at $500—insufficient for larger emergency expenses
Title loans require vehicle ownership and title possession, excluding renters and those with financed vehicles
Short repayment cycles on payday loans (2–4 weeks) create rollover/debt cycle risk
Checking account requirement excludes unbanked consumers
best_for: Employed workers needing cash between paychecks for small emergencies ($100–$500)
Vehicle owners with paid-off cars needing larger emergency funds ($2,000–$25,000)
Borrowers preferring in-person service and immediate funding over online-only lenders
Spanish-speaking consumers in Jacksonville seeking bilingual financial services
diagnosis: Advance America is best for employed consumers in immediate need of small emergency cash ($100–$1,000) or vehicle owners seeking larger collateral-based loans, with convenient same-day funding. The primary caveat is that payday and title loans carry high costs and short repayment terms that can trap borrowers in debt cycles; borrowers should explore installment loans or payday-alternative options (credit union PALs, employer advances) before committing to these products.
services: Installment loans ($100–$1,000 with flexible repayment schedules)
Title loans ($2,000–$25,000 secured by vehicle collateral)
In-store loan applications at physical locations
Personalized loan selection assistance from trained staff

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
